module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.label: Module 1 Product Starter Sheet Project
module-1-product-starter-sheet-project.summary: Build a simple product starter sheet that becomes clearer with each step.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.title: Preview the core product fields
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.prompt: Start the product sheet by returning `id`, `name`, and `category` from the `products` table.
mp-1-preview-product-sheet.hint: Add one more field to the working SELECT list.

help.concept: This step shapes the product sheet into a clearer report.
help.hint_1: Keep `id` and `name`, then add `category` before `FROM products`.
help.hint_2: Run the query and make sure the result shows the three requested columns.

mp-1-preview-product-sheet.starterCode:
-- Returning id, name, and category from the products table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM products;

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.title: Add the pricing field
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.prompt: Add `price` so the report shows category and price together.
mp-2-add-pricing-columns.hint: Extend the same query instead of rewriting it.

help.concept: Add the new field while keeping the parts of the sheet that already read well.
help.hint_2: Add `price` before `FROM products`, then rerun the query.

mp-2-add-pricing-columns.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add price before FROM.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category
FROM products;

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.title: Finish the starter sheet
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.prompt: Add `stock` so the final query returns `id`, `name`, `category`, `price`, and `stock`.
mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.hint: Insert the final field into the same SELECT list.

help.concept: This last step finishes the starter sheet with the final missing field.
help.hint_1: Do not remove any fields you already added.
help.hint_2: Add `stock` after `price`, then rerun the query.

mp-3-finish-product-starter-sheet.starterCode:
-- Keep the working query above.
-- Add stock after price.
SELECT
  id,
  name,
  category,
  price
FROM products;

try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.title: Query id and name from products
try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.prompt: Scenario: A store manager needs a clear product-catalog query before sharing results with the team. Write a query that returns `id`, `name`, `category` from the `products` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson, then adjust the selected columns, table, or filter for this scenario.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the selected columns and table name in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the result table with the expected result.

try-module-1-product-starter-sheet-proje.starterCode:
-- Return id, name, category from the products table.
SELECT
  id,
  name
FROM products;

try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.title: Query id and name from products
try_module_1_product_starter_sheet_proje.prompt: Scenario: A store manager needs a clear product-catalog query before sharing results with the team. Write a query that returns `id`, `name`, `category` from the `products` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
A teammate wants a starter product sheet that is easy to skim.

Begin with the core product details, then add the extra fields that make the sheet more useful.

The finished result should read like a simple report someone could actually use.
